items.title: Senior WA minister Paul Papalia resigns, triggering by-election

children.content: WA Labor stalwart and senior state cabinet minister Paul Papalia has announced he is quitting politics after nearly two decades, triggering a by-election in his southern Perth seat.

items.title: 'Disgusting' antisemitic graffiti sprayed on Anzac landmark

image.altText: 'Heil hitler' and a swastika sprayed on a wall of a historic bunker in Albany.

children.content: Swastikas and anti-Jewish abuse were discovered spray-painted on rocks and the historic Plantagenet Battery along Western Australia's south coast.

items.title: From Call of Duty to Dreamtime: These designers are lighting up video games

children.content: As part of NAIDOC Week, we meet the Indigenous creatives who are re-shaping Australia's video game industry.

image.altText: Two men stand with black t-shirts, one man on the left is playing a VR headset.

items.title: Declining vaccination rates behind whooping cough spike across WA's south

children.content: Whooping cough cases across WA's Great Southern have already more than doubled since last year, with doctors expecting the figure to grow further.

image.altText: A woman holds up a needle next to a vial.

items.title: Swimmer who disappeared from Perth beach 13 years ago likely killed by shark

children.content: Martyn Tann was last seen at Mullaloo Beach in 2013, with a coroner telling an inquest into his disappearance that she believed he was dead, likely in a shark attack.

items.title: Quick hits: England star hurt in celebrations, Haaland surprises himself

children.content: England revels in a famous victory over Mexico, but the celebrations come at a cost for one star, while Erling Haaland's brilliance even has the man himself dumbfounded. Here are today's World Cup quick hits.

articles.title: Belgium slams FIFA decision after Trump intervenes for US star

children.content: US striker Folarin Balogun is free to play Belgium despite earning a red card in his last match, in a shock decision that delights Donald Trump and "astonishes" the Belgians.

image.altText: Folarin Balogun points to his forehead in celebration

articles.title: Ice-cold Haaland triumphs again at World Cup, Brazil with a long road back

children.content: Brazil has long been trying to re-capture its glory days, but they are fading further into obscurity, while Erling Haaland proves Norway is a genuine World Cup threat in 2-1 win.

image.altText: Erling Haaland looks tough in celebration of a goal

articles.title: Gutsy England prevails over Mexico in instant World Cup classic

children.content: Ten-man England holds on to defeat Mexico 3-2 in a World Cup classic at the Azteca, with Jude Bellingham's brace and Harry Kane's penalty enough to send the Three Lions through.

image.altText: England players celebrate together, arm in arm

items.title: The world is getting pure Trump and the US presidency may never be the same

children.content: Donald Trump is undertaking regime change, not just in Venezuela, but on US soil and it is unlikely the presidency will ever be the same.

items.title: 'Not about race': Winmar statue removed two days after assault conviction

children.content: A statue immortalising a key moment in AFL history against racism is taken down, after its subject Nicky Winmar is found guilty of assaulting a woman.

items.title: NSW government announces review into boy's death amid cannibalism probe

children.content: Cannibalism will form part of an investigation into the death of a four-year-old boy on the NSW Central Coast, with an independent review to take place by the Department of Communities and Justice.

children.content: A record number of exchange traded funds were added to the Australian share market in the past financial year.

items.title: Rare barking geckos and Bungle Bungle sliders face extinction

children.content: New research has revealed the Pilbara barking gecko and Bungle Bungle robust slider are facing a heightened risk of extinction due to a rapidly warming climate.

items.title: Is a full day of screen time for kids ever OK?

children.content: Screen time has become a phrase that makes "parents feel instantly guilty", but how detrimental is a full day of screens on the odd occasion, like during school holidays or when you are home sick?

items.title: International experts weigh in on Australia’s bird flu response

children.content: While the H5N1 bird flu strain is relatively new to Australia, experts who have monitored the spread in other continents say surveillance is key to tackling the deadly virus.

items.title: 'Oldest continually practised medicine' being passed on to students

children.content: Aboriginal Australians have used native plants as bush medicine for tens of thousands of years. On Larrakia land, medical students are learning how nature can heal.

items.title: AFP to push Australia's policing model to UN

children.content: The AFP commissioner will promote Pacific police training as a model for the United Nations when she addresses police delegates from 140 nations this week.
